intro: Lasiocephalus senecioides, also known as Senecio, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its purported ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Lasiocephalus senecioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Lasiocephalus senecioides include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support lung health and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
